documentation:
This is the type of the message. "hang" is sent when the assistant is hanging due to a delay. The delay can be caused by many factors, such as:
- the model is too slow to respond
- the voice is too slow to respond
- the tool call is still waiting for a response from your server
- etc.

This matches what is stored on `call.artifact` after the call.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.server_message_hang.ServerMessageHang.assistant
documentation:
This is the assistant that is currently active. This is provided for convenience.

This matches one of the following:
- `call.assistant`,
- `call.assistantId`,
- `call.squad[n].assistant`,
- `call.squad[n].assistantId`,
- `call.squadId->[n].assistant`,
- `call.squadId->[n].assistantId`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.server_message_hang.ServerMessageHang.customer

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the tool.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.org_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the organization that this tool belongs to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.created_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was created.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.updated_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was last updated.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.output_tool.OutputTool.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.provider
documentation: This is the transcription provider that will be used.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.model
documentation: This is the Deepgram model that will be used. A list of models can be found here: https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/models-languages-overview

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.language
documentation: This is the language that will be set for the transcription. The list of languages Deepgram supports can be found here: https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/models-languages-overview

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.smart_format
documentation: This will be use smart format option provided by Deepgram. It's default disabled because it can sometimes format numbers as times but it's getting better.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.code_switching_enabled
documentation:
This automatically switches the transcriber's language when the customer's language changes. Defaults to false.

Usage:
- If your customers switch languages mid-call, you can set this to true.

Note:
- To detect language changes, Vapi uses a custom trained model. Languages supported (X = limited support):
  1. Arabic
  2. Bengali
  3. Cantonese
  4. Chinese
  5. Chinese Simplified (X)
  6. Chinese Traditional (X)
  7. English
  8. Farsi (X)
  9. French
  10. German
  11. Haitian Creole (X)
  12. Hindi
  13. Italian
  14. Japanese
  15. Korean
  16. Portuguese
  17. Russian
  18. Spanish
  19. Thai
  20. Urdu
  21. Vietnamese
- To receive `language-change-detected` webhook events, add it to `assistant.serverMessages`.

@default false

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.keywords
documentation: These keywords are passed to the transcription model to help it pick up use-case specific words. Anything that may not be a common word, like your company name, should be added here.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.endpointing
documentation:
This is the timeout after which Deepgram will send transcription on user silence. You can read in-depth documentation here: https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/endpointing.

Here are the most important bits:
- Defaults to 10. This is recommended for most use cases to optimize for latency.
- 10 can cause some missing transcriptions since because of the shorter context. This mostly happens for one-word utterances. For those uses cases, it's recommended to try 300. It will add a bit of latency but the quality and reliability of the experience will be better.
- If neither 10 nor 300 work, contact support@vapi.ai and we'll find another solution.

@default 10

class_variables.name: vapi.types.deepgram_transcriber.DeepgramTranscriber.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

documentation:
This is spoken to the customer before connecting them to the destination.

Usage:
- If this is not provided and transfer tool messages is not provided, default is "Transferring the call now".
- If set to "", nothing is spoken. This is useful when you want to silently transfer. This is especially useful when transferring between assistants in a squad. In this scenario, you likely also want to set `assistant.firstMessageMode=assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message` for the destination assistant.

This accepts a string or a ToolMessageStart class. Latter is useful if you want to specify multiple messages for different languages through the `contents` field.

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.model_based_condition.ModelBasedCondition.type
documentation: This condition is based on a model.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.model_based_condition.ModelBasedCondition.instruction
documentation:
This is the instruction which should output a boolean value when passed to a model.

You can reference any variable in the context of the current block execution (step):
- "{{output.your-property-name}}" for current step's output
- "{{input.your-property-name}}" for current step's input
- "{{your-step-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another step's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-step-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another step's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-block-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another block's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{your-block-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another block's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{workflow.input.your-property-name}}" for the current workflow's input
- "{{global.your-property-name}}" for the global context

You can also talk about the current step's output or input directly:
- "{{output.your-property-name}} is greater than 10"
- "{{input.your-property-name}} is greater than 10"

Examples:
 - "{{input.age}} is greater than 10"
 - "{{input.age}} is greater than {{input.age2}}"
 - "{{output.age}} is greater than 10"

Caveats:
1. a workflow can execute a step multiple times. example, if a loop is used in the graph. {{stepName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the step.
2. a workflow can execute a block multiple times. example, if a step is called multiple times or if a block is used in multiple steps. {{blockName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the block. this liquid variable is just provided for convenience when creating blocks outside of a workflow with steps.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.model_based_condition.ModelBasedCondition.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.update_org_dto.UpdateOrgDto.billing_limit

class_variables.name: vapi.types.update_org_dto.UpdateOrgDto.server_url

class_variables.name: vapi.types.update_org_dto.UpdateOrgDto.server_url_secret

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.fallback_destination
documentation:
This is the fallback destination an inbound call will be transferred to if:
1. `assistantId` is not set
2. `squadId` is not set
3. and, `assistant-request` message to the `serverUrl` fails

If this is not set and above conditions are met, the inbound call is hung up with an error message.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.area_code
documentation: This is the area code of the phone number to purchase.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.name
documentation: This is the name of the phone number. This is just for your own reference.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.assistant_id
documentation:
This is the assistant that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.squad_id
documentation:
This is the squad that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.server_url
documentation:
This is the server URL where messages will be sent for calls on this number. This includes the `assistant-request` message.

You can see the shape of the messages sent in `ServerMessage`.

This overrides the `org.serverUrl`. Order of precedence: tool.server.url > assistant.serverUrl > phoneNumber.serverUrl > org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.buy_phone_number_dto.BuyPhoneNumberDto.server_url_secret
documentation:
This is the secret Vapi will send with every message to your server. It's sent as a header called x-vapi-secret.

Same precedence logic as serverUrl.

modules.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto.CreateDtmfToolDto.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto.CreateDtmfToolDto.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto.CreateDtmfToolDto.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto.CreateDtmfToolDto.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_dtmf_tool_dto.CreateDtmfToolDto.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

In this scenario, you likely also want to set `assistant.firstMessageMode=assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message` for the destination assistant.

This accepts a string or a ToolMessageStart class. Latter is useful if you want to specify multiple messages for different languages through the `contents` field.

modules.name: vapi.types.transfer_plan
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_plan.TransferPlan.mode
documentation:
This configures how transfer is executed and the experience of the destination party receiving the call.

Usage:
- `blind-transfer`: The assistant forwards the call to the destination without any message or summary.
- `blind-transfer-add-summary-to-sip-header`: The assistant forwards the call to the destination and adds a SIP header X-Transfer-Summary to the call to include the summary.
- `warm-transfer-say-message`: The assistant dials the destination, delivers the `message` to the destination party, connects the customer, and leaves the call.
- `warm-transfer-say-summary`: The assistant dials the destination, provides a summary of the call to the destination party, connects the customer, and leaves the call.
- `warm-transfer-wait-for-operator-to-speak-first-and-then-say-message`: The assistant dials the destination, waits for the operator to speak, delivers the `message` to the destination party, and then connects the customer.
- `warm-transfer-wait-for-operator-to-speak-first-and-then-say-summary`: The assistant dials the destination, waits for the operator to speak, provides a summary of the call to the destination party, and then connects the customer.

@default 'blind-transfer'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_plan.TransferPlan.message
documentation:
This is the message the assistant will deliver to the destination party before connecting the customer.

Usage:
- Used only when `mode` is `warm-transfer-say-message` or `warm-transfer-wait-for-operator-to-speak-first-and-then-say-message`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_plan.TransferPlan.summary_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for generating a summary of the call to present to the destination party.

Usage:
- Used only when `mode` is `warm-transfer-say-summary` or `warm-transfer-wait-for-operator-to-speak-first-and-then-say-summary`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_plan.TransferPlan.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.first_message
documentation:
This is the first message that the assistant will say. This can also be a URL to a containerized audio file (mp3, wav, etc.).

If unspecified, assistant will wait for user to speak and use the model to respond once they speak.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.first_message_mode
documentation:
This is the mode for the first message. Default is 'assistant-speaks-first'.

Use:
- 'assistant-speaks-first' to have the assistant speak first.
- 'assistant-waits-for-user' to have the assistant wait for the user to speak first.
- 'assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message' to have the assistant speak first with a message generated by the model based on the conversation state. (`assistant.model.messages` at call start, `call.messages` at squad transfer points).

@default 'assistant-speaks-first'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.hipaa_enabled
documentation: When this is enabled, no logs, recordings, or transcriptions will be stored. At the end of the call, you will still receive an end-of-call-report message to store on your server. Defaults to false.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.client_messages
documentation: These are the messages that will be sent to your Client SDKs. Default is conversation-update,function-call,hang,model-output,speech-update,status-update,transfer-update,transcript,tool-calls,user-interrupted,voice-input. You can check the shape of the messages in ClientMessage schema.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.server_messages
documentation: These are the messages that will be sent to your Server URL. Default is conversation-update,end-of-call-report,function-call,hang,speech-update,status-update,tool-calls,transfer-destination-request,user-interrupted. You can check the shape of the messages in ServerMessage schema.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.silence_timeout_seconds
documentation:
How many seconds of silence to wait before ending the call. Defaults to 30.

@default 30

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.max_duration_seconds
documentation:
This is the maximum number of seconds that the call will last. When the call reaches this duration, it will be ended.

@default 600 (10 minutes)

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.background_sound
documentation: This is the background sound in the call. Default for phone calls is 'office' and default for web calls is 'off'.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.background_denoising_enabled
vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.model_output_in_messages_enabled
vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.transport_configurations
documentation: These are the configurations to be passed to the transport providers of assistant's calls, like Twilio. You can store multiple configurations for different transport providers. For a call, only the configuration matching the call transport provider is used.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.name
documentation:
This is the name of the assistant.

This is required when you want to transfer between assistants in a call.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.voicemail_detection
documentation:
These are the settings to configure or disable voicemail detection. Alternatively, voicemail detection can be configured using the model.tools=[VoicemailTool].
This uses Twilio's built-in detection while the VoicemailTool relies on the model to detect if a voicemail was reached.
You can use neither of them, one of them, or both of them. By default, Twilio built-in detection is enabled while VoicemailTool is not.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.voicemail_message
documentation:
This is the message that the assistant will say if the call is forwarded to voicemail.

If unspecified, it will hang up.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.end_call_message
documentation:
This is the message that the assistant will say if it ends the call.

If unspecified, it will hang up without saying anything.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.end_call_phrases
documentation: This list contains phrases that, if spoken by the assistant, will trigger the call to be hung up. Case insensitive.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.metadata
documentation: This is for metadata you want to store on the assistant.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.analysis_plan
documentation: This is the plan for analysis of assistant's calls. Stored in `call.analysis`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.artifact_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for artifacts generated during assistant's calls. Stored in `call.artifact`.

Note: `recordingEnabled` is currently at the root level. It will be moved to `artifactPlan` in the future, but will remain backwards compatible.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.message_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for static predefined messages that can be spoken by the assistant during the call, like `idleMessages`.

Note: `firstMessage`, `voicemailMessage`, and `endCallMessage` are currently at the root level. They will be moved to `messagePlan` in the future, but will remain backwards compatible.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.start_speaking_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for when the assistant should start talking.

You should configure this if you're running into these issues:
- The assistant is too slow to start talking after the customer is done speaking.
- The assistant is too fast to start talking after the customer is done speaking.
- The assistant is so fast that it's actually interrupting the customer.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.stop_speaking_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for when assistant should stop talking on customer interruption.

You should configure this if you're running into these issues:
- The assistant is too slow to recognize customer's interruption.
- The assistant is too fast to recognize customer's interruption.
- The assistant is getting interrupted by phrases that are just acknowledgments.
- The assistant is getting interrupted by background noises.
- The assistant is not properly stopping -- it starts talking right after getting interrupted.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.monitor_plan
documentation:
This is the plan for real-time monitoring of the assistant's calls.

Usage:
- To enable live listening of the assistant's calls, set `monitorPlan.listenEnabled` to `true`.
- To enable live control of the assistant's calls, set `monitorPlan.controlEnabled` to `true`.

Note, `serverMessages`, `clientMessages`, `serverUrl` and `serverUrlSecret` are currently at the root level but will be moved to `monitorPlan` in the future. Will remain backwards compatible

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.credential_ids
documentation: These are the credentials that will be used for the assistant calls. By default, all the credentials are available for use in the call but you can provide a subset using this.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.server
documentation:
This is where Vapi will send webhooks. You can find all webhooks available along with their shape in ServerMessage schema.

The order of precedence is:

1. assistant.server.url
2. phoneNumber.serverUrl
3. org.serverUrl

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the assistant.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.org_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the org that this assistant belongs to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.created_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the assistant was created.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.updated_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the assistant was last updated.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant.Assistant.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.message
documentation:
This is the message that will be spoken to the user.

If this is not returned, assistant will speak:
1. a `request-complete` or `request-failed` message from `tool.messages`, if it exists
2. a response generated by the model, if not

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.name
documentation: This is the name of the function the model called.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.tool_call_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the tool call.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.result
documentation:
This is the result if the tool call was successful. This is added to the conversation history.

Further, if this is returned, assistant will speak:
1. the `message`, if it exists and is of type `request-complete`
2. a `request-complete` message from `tool.messages`, if it exists
3. a response generated by the model, if neither exist

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.error
documentation:
This is the error if the tool call was not successful. This is added to the conversation history.

Further, if this is returned, assistant will speak:
1. the `message`, if it exists and is of type `request-failed`
2. a `request-failed` message from `tool.messages`, if it exists
3. a response generated by the model, if neither exist

class_variables.name: vapi.types.tool_call_result.ToolCallResult.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_make_tool_dto.CreateMakeToolDto.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_make_tool_dto.CreateMakeToolDto.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_make_tool_dto.CreateMakeToolDto.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_make_tool_dto.CreateMakeToolDto.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_make_tool_dto.CreateMakeToolDto.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.number_e_164_check_enabled
documentation:
This is the flag to toggle the E164 check for the `number` field. This is an advanced property which should be used if you know your use case requires it.

Use cases:
- `false`: To allow non-E164 numbers like `+001234567890`, `1234`, or `abc`. This is useful for dialing out to non-E164 numbers on your SIP trunks.
- `true` (default): To allow only E164 numbers like `+14155551234`. This is standard for PSTN calls.

If `false`, the `number` is still required to only contain alphanumeric characters (regex: `/^\+?[a-zA-Z0-9]+$/`).

@default true (E164 check is enabled)

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.extension
documentation: This is the extension that will be dialed after the call is answered.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.number
documentation: This is the number of the customer.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.sip_uri
documentation: This is the SIP URI of the customer.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.name
documentation:
This is the name of the customer. This is just for your own reference.

For SIP inbound calls, this is extracted from the `From` SIP header with format `"Display Name" <sip:username@domain>`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_customer_dto.CreateCustomerDto.model_config

modules.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.type
documentation: The type of tool. "voicemail". This uses the model itself to determine if a voicemil was reached. Can be used alternatively/alongside with TwilioVoicemailDetection

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_voicemail_tool_dto.CreateVoicemailToolDto.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.org
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.hipaa_enabled
documentation:
When this is enabled, no logs, recordings, or transcriptions will be stored. At the end of the call, you will still receive an end-of-call-report message to store on your server. Defaults to false.
When HIPAA is enabled, only OpenAI/Custom LLM or Azure Providers will be available for LLM and Voice respectively.
This is due to the compliance requirements of HIPAA. Other providers may not meet these requirements.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.channel
documentation: This is the channel of the org. There is the cluster the API traffic for the org will be directed.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.billing_limit
documentation: This is the monthly billing limit for the org. To go beyond $1000/mo, please contact us at support@vapi.ai.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.server_url
documentation:
This is the URL Vapi will communicate with via HTTP GET and POST Requests. This is used for retrieving context, function calling, and end-of-call reports.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things relevant to that message. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.server_url_secret
documentation: This is the secret you can set that Vapi will send with every request to your server. Will be sent as a header called x-vapi-secret.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.org.Org.concurrency_limit
documentation: This is the concurrency limit for the org. This is the maximum number of calls that can be active at any given time. To go beyond 10, please contact us at support@vapi.ai.

- `call.assistantId->voice`,
- `call.squad[n].assistant.voice`,

modules.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.message
documentation:
This is spoken to the customer before connecting them to the destination.

Usage:
- If this is not provided and transfer tool messages is not provided, default is "Transferring the call now".
- If set to "", nothing is spoken. This is useful when you want to silently transfer. This is especially useful when transferring between assistants in a squad. In this scenario, you likely also want to set `assistant.firstMessageMode=assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message` for the destination assistant.

This accepts a string or a ToolMessageStart class. Latter is useful if you want to specify multiple messages for different languages through the `contents` field.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.number_e_164_check_enabled
documentation:
This is the flag to toggle the E164 check for the `number` field. This is an advanced property which should be used if you know your use case requires it.

Use cases:
- `false`: To allow non-E164 numbers like `+001234567890`, `1234`, or `abc`. This is useful for dialing out to non-E164 numbers on your SIP trunks.
- `true` (default): To allow only E164 numbers like `+14155551234`. This is standard for PSTN calls.

If `false`, the `number` is still required to only contain alphanumeric characters (regex: `/^\+?[a-zA-Z0-9]+$/`).

@default true (E164 check is enabled)

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.number
documentation: This is the phone number to transfer the call to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.extension
documentation: This is the extension to dial after transferring the call to the `number`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.caller_id
documentation:
This is the caller ID to use when transferring the call to the `number`.

Usage:
- If not provided, the caller ID will be the number the call is coming from. Example, +14151111111 calls in to and the assistant transfers out to +16470000000. +16470000000 will see +14151111111 as the caller.
- To change this behavior, provide a `callerId`.
- Set to '{{customer.number}}' to always use the customer's number as the caller ID.
- Set to '{{phoneNumber.number}}' to always use the phone number of the assistant as the caller ID.
- Set to any E164 number to always use that number as the caller ID. This needs to be a number that is owned or verified by your Transport provider like Twilio.

For Twilio, you can read up more here: https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/twiml/dial#callerid

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.transfer_plan
documentation:
This configures how transfer is executed and the experience of the destination party receiving the call. Defaults to `blind-transfer`.

@default `transferPlan.mode='blind-transfer'`

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.description
documentation: This is the description of the destination, used by the AI to choose when and how to transfer the call.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_number.TransferDestinationNumber.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.type
documentation:
This is the type of output you'd like.

`string`, `number`, `integer`, `boolean` are the primitive types and should be obvious.

`array` and `object` are more interesting and quite powerful. They allow you to define nested structures.

For `array`, you can define the schema of the items in the array using the `items` property.

For `object`, you can define the properties of the object using the `properties` property.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.items
documentation:
This is required if the type is "array". This is the schema of the items in the array.

This is of type JsonSchema. However, Swagger doesn't support circular references.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.properties
documentation:
This is required if the type is "object". This specifies the properties of the object.

This is a map of string to JsonSchema. However, Swagger doesn't support circular references.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.description
documentation: This is the description to help the model understand what it needs to output.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.required
documentation:
This is a list of properties that are required.

This only makes sense if the type is "object".

class_variables.name: vapi.types.json_schema.JsonSchema.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

The operation becomes `(leftSide) operator (rightSide)`.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.rule_based_condition.RuleBasedCondition.left_side
documentation:
This is the left side of the operation.

You can reference any variable in the context of the current block execution (step):
- "{{output.your-property-name}}" for current step's output
- "{{input.your-property-name}}" for current step's input
- "{{your-step-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another step's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-step-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another step's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-block-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another block's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{your-block-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another block's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{workflow.input.your-property-name}}" for the current workflow's input
- "{{global.your-property-name}}" for the global context

Or, you can use a constant:
- "1"
- "text"
- "true"
- "false"

Or, you can mix and match with string interpolation:
- "{{your-property-name}}-{{input.your-property-name-2}}-1"

Caveats:
1. a workflow can execute a step multiple times. example, if a loop is used in the graph. {{stepName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the step.
2. a workflow can execute a block multiple times. example, if a step is called multiple times or if a block is used in multiple steps. {{blockName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the block. this liquid variable is just provided for convenience when creating blocks outside of a workflow with steps.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.rule_based_condition.RuleBasedCondition.right_side
documentation:
This is the right side of the operation.

You can reference any variable in the context of the current block execution (step):
- "{{output.your-property-name}}" for current step's output
- "{{input.your-property-name}}" for current step's input
- "{{your-step-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another step's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-step-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another step's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #1)
- "{{your-block-name.output.your-property-name}}" for another block's output (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{your-block-name.input.your-property-name}}" for another block's input (in the same workflow; read caveat #2)
- "{{workflow.input.your-property-name}}" for the current workflow's input
- "{{global.your-property-name}}" for the global context

Or, you can use a constant:
- "1"
- "text"
- "true"
- "false"

Or, you can mix and match with string interpolation:
- "{{your-property-name}}-{{input.your-property-name-2}}-1"

Caveats:
1. a workflow can execute a step multiple times. example, if a loop is used in the graph. {{stepName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the step.
2. a workflow can execute a block multiple times. example, if a step is called multiple times or if a block is used in multiple steps. {{blockName.input/output.propertyName}} will reference the latest usage of the block. this liquid variable is just provided for convenience when creating blocks outside of a workflow with steps.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.artifact_plan.ArtifactPlan.recording_enabled
documentation:
This determines whether assistant's calls are recorded. Defaults to true.

Usage:
- If you don't want to record the calls, set this to false.
- If you want to record the calls when `assistant.hipaaEnabled`, explicity set this to true and make sure to provide S3 or GCP credentials on the Provider Credentials page in the Dashboard.

You can find the recording at `call.artifact.recordingUrl` and `call.artifact.stereoRecordingUrl` after the call is ended.

@default true

class_variables.name: vapi.types.artifact_plan.ArtifactPlan.video_recording_enabled
vapi.types.artifact_plan.ArtifactPlan.transcript_plan
documentation: This is the plan for `call.artifact.transcript`. To disable, set `transcriptPlan.enabled` to false.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.artifact_plan.ArtifactPlan.recording_path
documentation:
This is the path where the recording will be uploaded. This is only used if you have provided S3 or GCP credentials on the Provider Credentials page in the Dashboard.

If credential.s3PathPrefix or credential.bucketPlan.path is set, this will append to it.

Usage:
- If you want to upload the recording to a specific path, set this to the path. Example: `/my-assistant-recordings`.
- If you want to upload the recording to the root of the bucket, set this to `/`.

@default '/'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.artifact_plan.ArtifactPlan.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.message_plan.MessagePlan.idle_messages
documentation:
This are the messages that the assistant will speak when the user hasn't responded for `idleTimeoutSeconds`. Each time the timeout is triggered, a random message will be chosen from this array.

Usage:
- If user gets distracted and doesn't respond for a while, this can be used to grab their attention.
- If the transcriber doesn't pick up what the user said, this can be used to ask the user to repeat themselves. (From the perspective of the assistant, the conversation is idle since it didn't "hear" any user messages.)

@default null (no idle message is spoken)

class_variables.name: vapi.types.message_plan.MessagePlan.idle_timeout_seconds

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.fallback_destination
documentation:
This is the fallback destination an inbound call will be transferred to if:
1. `assistantId` is not set
2. `squadId` is not set
3. and, `assistant-request` message to the `serverUrl` fails

If this is not set and above conditions are met, the inbound call is hung up with an error message.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the phone number.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.org_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the org that this phone number belongs to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.created_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the phone number was created.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.updated_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the phone number was last updated.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.name
documentation: This is the name of the phone number. This is just for your own reference.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.assistant_id
documentation:
This is the assistant that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.squad_id
documentation:
This is the squad that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.server_url
documentation:
This is the server URL where messages will be sent for calls on this number. This includes the `assistant-request` message.

You can see the shape of the messages sent in `ServerMessage`.

This overrides the `org.serverUrl`. Order of precedence: tool.server.url > assistant.serverUrl > phoneNumber.serverUrl > org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.server_url_secret
documentation:
This is the secret Vapi will send with every message to your server. It's sent as a header called x-vapi-secret.

Same precedence logic as serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.sip_uri
documentation:
This is the SIP URI of the phone number. You can SIP INVITE this. The assistant attached to this number will answer.

This is case-insensitive.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.vapi_phone_number.VapiPhoneNumber.authentication
documentation:
This enables authentication for incoming SIP INVITE requests to the `sipUri`.

If not set, any username/password to the 401 challenge of the SIP INVITE will be accepted.

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.ghl_tool_with_tool_call.GhlToolWithToolCall.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.ghl_tool_with_tool_call.GhlToolWithToolCall.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.ghl_tool_with_tool_call.GhlToolWithToolCall.type
documentation: The type of tool. "ghl" for GHL tool.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.ghl_tool_with_tool_call.GhlToolWithToolCall.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.ghl_tool_with_tool_call.GhlToolWithToolCall.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

- If you want to change the name of the assistant in the transcript, set this. Example, here is what the transcript would look like with `assistantName` set to 'Buyer':
```
User: Hello, how are you?
Buyer: I'm fine.
User: Do you want to buy a car?
Buyer: No.
```

@default 'AI'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transcript_plan.TranscriptPlan.user_name
documentation:
This is the name of the user in the transcript. Defaults to 'User'.

Usage:
- If you want to change the name of the user in the transcript, set this. Example, here is what the transcript would look like with `userName` set to 'Seller':
```
Seller: Hello, how are you?
AI: I'm fine.
Seller: Do you want to buy a car?
AI: No.
```

@default 'User'

modules.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.provider
documentation: This is the provider to use for voicemail detection.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.voicemail_detection_types
documentation:
These are the AMD messages from Twilio that are considered as voicemail. Default is ['machine_end_beep', 'machine_end_silence'].

@default {Array} ['machine_end_beep', 'machine_end_silence']

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.enabled
documentation:
This sets whether the assistant should detect voicemail. Defaults to true.

@default true

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.machine_detection_timeout
documentation:
The number of seconds that Twilio should attempt to perform answering machine detection before timing out and returning AnsweredBy as unknown. Default is 30 seconds.

Increasing this value will provide the engine more time to make a determination. This can be useful when DetectMessageEnd is provided in the MachineDetection parameter and there is an expectation of long answering machine greetings that can exceed 30 seconds.

Decreasing this value will reduce the amount of time the engine has to make a determination. This can be particularly useful when the Enable option is provided in the MachineDetection parameter and you want to limit the time for initial detection.

Check the [Twilio docs](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection#optional-api-tuning-parameters) for more info.

@default 30

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.machine_detection_speech_threshold
documentation:
The number of milliseconds that is used as the measuring stick for the length of the speech activity. Durations lower than this value will be interpreted as a human, longer as a machine. Default is 2400 milliseconds.

Increasing this value will reduce the chance of a False Machine (detected machine, actually human) for a long human greeting (e.g., a business greeting) but increase the time it takes to detect a machine.

Decreasing this value will reduce the chances of a False Human (detected human, actually machine) for short voicemail greetings. The value of this parameter may need to be reduced by more than 1000ms to detect very short voicemail greetings. A reduction of that significance can result in increased False Machine detections. Adjusting the MachineDetectionSpeechEndThreshold is likely the better approach for short voicemails. Decreasing MachineDetectionSpeechThreshold will also reduce the time it takes to detect a machine.

Check the [Twilio docs](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection#optional-api-tuning-parameters) for more info.

@default 2400

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.machine_detection_speech_end_threshold
documentation:
The number of milliseconds of silence after speech activity at which point the speech activity is considered complete. Default is 1200 milliseconds.

Increasing this value will typically be used to better address the short voicemail greeting scenarios. For short voicemails, there is typically 1000-2000ms of audio followed by 1200-2400ms of silence and then additional audio before the beep. Increasing the MachineDetectionSpeechEndThreshold to ~2500ms will treat the 1200-2400ms of silence as a gap in the greeting but not the end of the greeting and will result in a machine detection. The downsides of such a change include:
- Increasing the delay for human detection by the amount you increase this parameter, e.g., a change of 1200ms to 2500ms increases human detection delay by 1300ms.
- Cases where a human has two utterances separated by a period of silence (e.g. a "Hello", then 2000ms of silence, and another "Hello") may be interpreted as a machine.

Decreasing this value will result in faster human detection. The consequence is that it can lead to increased False Human (detected human, actually machine) detections because a silence gap in a voicemail greeting (not necessarily just in short voicemail scenarios) can be incorrectly interpreted as the end of speech.

Check the [Twilio docs](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection#optional-api-tuning-parameters) for more info.

@default 1200

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.machine_detection_silence_timeout
documentation:
The number of milliseconds of initial silence after which an unknown AnsweredBy result will be returned. Default is 5000 milliseconds.

Increasing this value will result in waiting for a longer period of initial silence before returning an 'unknown' AMD result.

Decreasing this value will result in waiting for a shorter period of initial silence before returning an 'unknown' AMD result.

Check the [Twilio docs](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/answering-machine-detection#optional-api-tuning-parameters) for more info.

@default 5000

class_variables.name: vapi.types.twilio_voicemail_detection.TwilioVoicemailDetection.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule.CustomerCustomEndpointingRule.type
documentation:
This endpointing rule is based on current customer message as they are speaking.

Flow:
- Assistant speaks
- Customer starts speaking
- Customer transcription comes in
- This rule is evaluated on the current customer transcription
- If a match is found based on `regex`, the endpointing timeout is set to `timeoutSeconds`

Usage:
- If you want to wait longer while customer is speaking numbers, you can set a longer timeout.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule.CustomerCustomEndpointingRule.regex
documentation:
This is the regex pattern to match.

Note:
- This works by using the `RegExp.test` method in Node.JS. Eg. `/hello/.test("hello there")` will return `true`.

Hot tip:
- In JavaScript, escape `\` when sending the regex pattern. Eg. `"hello\sthere"` will be sent over the wire as `"hellosthere"`. Send `"hello\sthere"` instead.
- `RegExp.test` does substring matching, so `/cat/.test("I love cats")` will return `true`. To do full string matching, send "^cat$".

class_variables.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule.CustomerCustomEndpointingRule.regex_options
documentation:
These are the options for the regex match. Defaults to all disabled.

@default []

class_variables.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule.CustomerCustomEndpointingRule.timeout_seconds
documentation: This is the endpointing timeout in seconds, if the rule is matched.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.customer_custom_endpointing_rule.CustomerCustomEndpointingRule.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.fallback_destination
documentation:
This is the fallback destination an inbound call will be transferred to if:
1. `assistantId` is not set
2. `squadId` is not set
3. and, `assistant-request` message to the `serverUrl` fails

If this is not set and above conditions are met, the inbound call is hung up with an error message.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.vonage_phone_number
documentation: These are the digits of the phone number you own on your Vonage.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.credential_id
documentation:
This is the credential that is used to make outgoing calls, and do operations like call transfer and hang up.

You can add the Vonage Credential in the Provider Credentials page on the dashboard to get the credentialId.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.name
documentation: This is the name of the phone number. This is just for your own reference.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.assistant_id
documentation:
This is the assistant that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.squad_id
documentation:
This is the squad that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.server_url
documentation:
This is the server URL where messages will be sent for calls on this number. This includes the `assistant-request` message.

You can see the shape of the messages sent in `ServerMessage`.

This overrides the `org.serverUrl`. Order of precedence: tool.server.url > assistant.serverUrl > phoneNumber.serverUrl > org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.server_url_secret
documentation:
This is the secret Vapi will send with every message to your server. It's sent as a header called x-vapi-secret.

Same precedence logic as serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.import_vonage_phone_number_dto.ImportVonagePhoneNumberDto.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

class_variables.name: vapi.types.custom_message.CustomMessage.contents
documentation:
This is an alternative to the `content` property. It allows to specify variants of the same content, one per language.

Usage:
- If your assistants are multilingual, you can provide content for each language.
- If you don't provide content for a language, the first item in the array will be automatically translated to the active language at that moment.

This will override the `content` property.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the tool.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.org_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the organization that this tool belongs to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.created_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was created.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.updated_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was last updated.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.make_tool.MakeTool.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant_custom_endpointing_rule.AssistantCustomEndpointingRule.type
documentation:
This endpointing rule is based on the last assistant message before customer started speaking.

Flow:
- Assistant speaks
- Customer starts speaking
- Customer transcription comes in
- This rule is evaluated on the last assistant message
- If a match is found based on `regex`, the endpointing timeout is set to `timeoutSeconds`

Usage:
- If you have yes/no questions in your use case like "are you interested in a loan?", you can set a shorter timeout.
- If you have questions where the customer may pause to look up information like "what's my account number?", you can set a longer timeout.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant_custom_endpointing_rule.AssistantCustomEndpointingRule.regex
documentation:
This is the regex pattern to match.

Note:
- This works by using the `RegExp.test` method in Node.JS. Eg. `/hello/.test("hello there")` will return `true`.

Hot tip:
- In JavaScript, escape `\` when sending the regex pattern. Eg. `"hello\sthere"` will be sent over the wire as `"hellosthere"`. Send `"hello\sthere"` instead.
- `RegExp.test` does substring matching, so `/cat/.test("I love cats")` will return `true`. To do full string matching, send "^cat$".

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant_custom_endpointing_rule.AssistantCustomEndpointingRule.regex_options
documentation:
These are the options for the regex match. Defaults to all disabled.

@default []

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant_custom_endpointing_rule.AssistantCustomEndpointingRule.timeout_seconds
documentation: This is the endpointing timeout in seconds, if the rule is matched.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.assistant_custom_endpointing_rule.AssistantCustomEndpointingRule.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.bash_tool
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.async_
documentation:
This determines if the tool is async.

If async, the assistant will move forward without waiting for your server to respond. This is useful if you just want to trigger something on your server.

If sync, the assistant will wait for your server to respond. This is useful if want assistant to respond with the result from your server.

Defaults to synchronous (`false`).

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.messages
documentation:
These are the messages that will be spoken to the user as the tool is running.

For some tools, this is auto-filled based on special fields like `tool.destinations`. For others like the function tool, these can be custom configured.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.sub_type
documentation: The sub type of tool.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the tool.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.org_id
documentation: This is the unique identifier for the organization that this tool belongs to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.created_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was created.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.updated_at
documentation: This is the ISO 8601 date-time string of when the tool was last updated.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.function
documentation:
This is the function definition of the tool.

For `endCall`, `transferCall`, and `dtmf` tools, this is auto-filled based on tool-specific fields like `tool.destinations`. But, even in those cases, you can provide a custom function definition for advanced use cases.

An example of an advanced use case is if you want to customize the message that's spoken for `endCall` tool. You can specify a function where it returns an argument "reason". Then, in `messages` array, you can have many "request-complete" messages. One of these messages will be triggered if the `messages[].conditions` matches the "reason" argument.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.server
documentation:
This is the server that will be hit when this tool is requested by the model.

All requests will be sent with the call object among other things. You can find more details in the Server URL documentation.

This overrides the serverUrl set on the org and the phoneNumber. Order of precedence: highest tool.server.url, then assistant.serverUrl, then phoneNumber.serverUrl, then org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.name
documentation: The name of the tool, fixed to 'bash'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.bash_tool.BashTool.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.fallback_destination
documentation:
This is the fallback destination an inbound call will be transferred to if:
1. `assistantId` is not set
2. `squadId` is not set
3. and, `assistant-request` message to the `serverUrl` fails

If this is not set and above conditions are met, the inbound call is hung up with an error message.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.number_e_164_check_enabled
documentation:
This is the flag to toggle the E164 check for the `number` field. This is an advanced property which should be used if you know your use case requires it.

Use cases:
- `false`: To allow non-E164 numbers like `+001234567890`, `1234`, or `abc`. This is useful for dialing out to non-E164 numbers on your SIP trunks.
- `true` (default): To allow only E164 numbers like `+14155551234`. This is standard for PSTN calls.

If `false`, the `number` is still required to only contain alphanumeric characters (regex: `/^\+?[a-zA-Z0-9]+$/`).

@default true (E164 check is enabled)

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.number
documentation: This is the number of the customer.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.credential_id
documentation:
This is the credential of your own SIP trunk or Carrier (type `byo-sip-trunk`) which can be used to make calls to this phone number.

You can add the SIP trunk or Carrier credential in the Provider Credentials page on the Dashboard to get the credentialId.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.name
documentation: This is the name of the phone number. This is just for your own reference.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.assistant_id
documentation:
This is the assistant that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.squad_id
documentation:
This is the squad that will be used for incoming calls to this phone number.

If neither `assistantId` nor `squadId` is set, `assistant-request` will be sent to your Server URL. Check `ServerMessage` and `ServerMessageResponse` for the shape of the message and response that is expected.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.server_url
documentation:
This is the server URL where messages will be sent for calls on this number. This includes the `assistant-request` message.

You can see the shape of the messages sent in `ServerMessage`.

This overrides the `org.serverUrl`. Order of precedence: tool.server.url > assistant.serverUrl > phoneNumber.serverUrl > org.serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.server_url_secret
documentation:
This is the secret Vapi will send with every message to your server. It's sent as a header called x-vapi-secret.

Same precedence logic as serverUrl.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.create_byo_phone_number_dto.CreateByoPhoneNumberDto.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant.TransferDestinationAssistant.message
documentation:
This is spoken to the customer before connecting them to the destination.

Usage:
- If this is not provided and transfer tool messages is not provided, default is "Transferring the call now".
- If set to "", nothing is spoken. This is useful when you want to silently transfer. This is especially useful when transferring between assistants in a squad. In this scenario, you likely also want to set `assistant.firstMessageMode=assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message` for the destination assistant.

This accepts a string or a ToolMessageStart class. Latter is useful if you want to specify multiple messages for different languages through the `contents` field.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant.TransferDestinationAssistant.transfer_mode
documentation:
This is the mode to use for the transfer. Defaults to `rolling-history`.

- `rolling-history`: This is the default mode. It keeps the entire conversation history and appends the new assistant's system message on transfer.

  Example:

  Pre-transfer:
    system: assistant1 system message
    assistant: assistant1 first message
    user: hey, good morning
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account
    assistant: (destination.message)

  Post-transfer:
    system: assistant1 system message
    assistant: assistant1 first message
    user: hey, good morning
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account
    assistant: (destination.message)
    system: assistant2 system message
    assistant: assistant2 first message (or model generated if firstMessageMode is set to `assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message`)

- `swap-system-message-in-history`: This replaces the original system message with the new assistant's system message on transfer.

  Example:

  Pre-transfer:
    system: assistant1 system message
    assistant: assistant1 first message
    user: hey, good morning
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account
    assistant: (destination.message)

  Post-transfer:
    system: assistant2 system message
    assistant: assistant1 first message
    user: hey, good morning
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account
    assistant: (destination.message)
    assistant: assistant2 first message (or model generated if firstMessageMode is set to `assistant-speaks-first-with-model-generated-message`)

- `delete-history`: This deletes the entire conversation history on transfer.

  Example:

  Pre-transfer:
    system: assistant1 system message
    assistant: assistant1 first message
    user: hey, good morning
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account
    assistant: (destination.message)

  Post-transfer:
    system: assistant2 system message
    assistant: assistant2 first message
    user: Yes, please
    assistant: how can i help?
    user: i need help with my account

@default 'rolling-history'

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant.TransferDestinationAssistant.assistant_name
documentation: This is the assistant to transfer the call to.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant.TransferDestinationAssistant.description
documentation: This is the description of the destination, used by the AI to choose when and how to transfer the call.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.transfer_destination_assistant.TransferDestinationAssistant.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2

modules.name: vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan
documentation: This file was auto-generated by Fern from our API Definition.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan.SuccessEvaluationPlan.rubric
documentation:
This enforces the rubric of the evaluation. The output is stored in `call.analysis.successEvaluation`.

Options include:
- 'NumericScale': A scale of 1 to 10.
- 'DescriptiveScale': A scale of Excellent, Good, Fair, Poor.
- 'Checklist': A checklist of criteria and their status.
- 'Matrix': A grid that evaluates multiple criteria across different performance levels.
- 'PercentageScale': A scale of 0% to 100%.
- 'LikertScale': A scale of Strongly Agree, Agree, Neutral, Disagree, Strongly Disagree.
- 'AutomaticRubric': Automatically break down evaluation into several criteria, each with its own score.
- 'PassFail': A simple 'true' if call passed, 'false' if not.

Default is 'PassFail'.

class_variables.name: vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan.SuccessEvaluationPlan.messages
vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan.SuccessEvaluationPlan.enabled
documentation:
This determines whether a success evaluation is generated and stored in `call.analysis.successEvaluation`. Defaults to true.

Usage:
- If you want to disable the success evaluation, set this to false.

@default true

class_variables.name: vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan.SuccessEvaluationPlan.timeout_seconds
documentation:
This is how long the request is tried before giving up. When request times out, `call.analysis.successEvaluation` will be empty.

Usage:
- To guarantee the success evaluation is generated, set this value high. Note, this will delay the end of call report in cases where model is slow to respond.

@default 5 seconds

class_variables.name: vapi.types.success_evaluation_plan.SuccessEvaluationPlan.model_config
documentation: type: ignore # Pydantic v2
